title: Nehemiah 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 These are the words of Nehemiah son of Hacaliah. In the month of Chislev, in the twentieth year, I was in the palace of Susa.
2 Hanani, one of my brothers, came with some men from Judah. I asked them about the Jews who were left after the exile, and also about Jerusalem.
3 They told me, "Those who were left from the exile are there in the region. They are in great trouble and shame. The wall of Jerusalem is broken down, and its gates are burned with fire."
4 When I heard these words, I sat down and cried. I was sad for many days. I did not eat and I prayed before the God of heaven.
5 Then I said, "O LORD, God of heaven, You are the great and feared God. You keep Your covenant of faithful love with those who love You and keep Your commandments.

6 Let Your eyes be open and Your ears ready to hear. Hear the prayer that I, Your servant, now pray before You day and night for Your servants, the Israelites. I admit the sins that we Israelites have done against You. Both I and my father's house have sinned.
7 We have acted very badly against You. We have not kept the commandments, rules, and laws that You gave Your servant Moses.
8 Remember, I pray, the word that You gave Your servant Moses. You said, 'If you are not faithful to Me, I will spread you among the nations.

9 But if you return to Me and keep and do My commandments, I will gather you. Even if your exiles are sent to the farthest place on earth, I will bring them back. I will bring them to the place I have chosen as a home for My Name.'
10 They are Your servants and Your people. You bought them back by Your great power and strong hand.

11 O Lord, may Your ear be ready to hear my prayer. Hear the prayers of Your servants who are glad to honor Your name. Give Your servant success today, I pray. Give him mercy in the sight of this man." At that time I was the cupbearer to the king.

title: Nehemiah 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 It was the month of Nisan, in the twentieth year of King Artaxerxes. Wine was set before the king, and I took it and gave it to him. I had never looked sad in front of him before.
2 So the king said to me, "Why is your face sad? You are not ill. This must be sadness of the heart." I was very afraid.
3 I said to the king, "May the king live forever! Why should I not be sad? The city where my fathers are buried is broken down, and its gates are burned with fire."
4 The king replied, "What do you ask for?" So I prayed to the God of heaven.
5 Then I answered the king, "If it pleases the king, and if you are pleased with your servant, please send me to Judah. Send me to the city where my fathers are buried, so that I may rebuild it."
6 The queen was sitting beside the king. He asked me, "How long will your trip take, and when will you return?" It pleased the king to send me, and I agreed on a time with him.
7 I also said to him, "If it pleases the king, please give me letters for the governors of the lands west of the Euphrates River. Let them allow me to pass safely until I reach Judah.

8 And give me a letter for Asaph, the man in charge of the king's forest. Tell him to give me wood to make beams for the gates of the fort next to the temple, for the city wall, and for the house I will live in." My God was kind to me and helped me, and the king gave me what I asked.
9 Then I went to the governors of the lands west of the Euphrates and gave them the king's letters. The king had also sent army officers and horsemen with me.
10 But Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite official heard about this. They were very upset that someone had come to help the people of Israel.
11 I arrived in Jerusalem and stayed there three days.
12 Then I set out at night with a few men. I did not tell anyone what my God had put in my heart to do for Jerusalem. The only animal with me was the one I was riding.

13 So I went out at night through the Valley Gate. I went toward the Well of the Serpent and the Dung Gate. I looked carefully at the walls of Jerusalem that were broken down. I also looked at the gates that fire had destroyed.
14 Then I went on to the Fountain Gate and the King's Pool. But there was no space for my donkey to pass under me.
15 So I went up the valley by night and looked carefully at the wall. Then I turned back and entered again through the Valley Gate.
16 The officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing. I had not yet told the Jews or the priests or the nobles or the officials or any other workers.
17 Then I said to them, "You see the trouble we are in. Jerusalem is broken down, and its gates are burned with fire. Come, let us rebuild the wall of Jerusalem. Then we will no longer be in shame."
18 I also told them how my God had been kind to me and helped me. I told them what the king had said. They replied, "Let us start rebuilding." So they started this good work.

19 But Sanballat the Horonite, Tobiah the Ammonite official, and Geshem the Arab heard about this. They laughed at us and made fun of us. They said, "What is this you are doing? Are you turning against the king?"
20 So I answered them, "The God of heaven will give us success. We are His servants, and we will start rebuilding. But you have no share, no right, and no claim in Jerusalem."

title: Nehemiah 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 At the Sheep Gate, Eliashib the high priest and the other priests started to rebuild. They set it apart for God and put its doors in place. They built as far as the Tower of the Hundred and the Tower of Hananel, and they set apart the wall for God.
2 The men of Jericho built next to Eliashib. Zaccur son of Imri built next to them.
3 The sons of Hassenaah rebuilt the Fish Gate. They laid its beams and put its doors, locks, and bars in place.

4 Next to them, Meremoth son of Uriah, the son of Hakkoz, repaired the wall. Next to him, Meshullam son of Berechiah, the son of Meshezabel, repaired the wall. Next to him, Zadok son of Baana also repaired the wall.
5 Next to him, the Tekoites repaired the wall. But their nobles did not do the hard work for their leaders.
6 Joiada son of Paseah and Meshullam son of Besodeiah repaired the Jeshanah Gate. They laid its beams and put its doors, locks, and bars in place.
7 Next to them, Melatiah the Gibeonite and Jadon the Meronothite made repairs. The men of Gibeon and Mizpah helped them. These men were under the governor of the lands west of the Euphrates.

8 Next to them, Uzziel son of Harhaiah, one of the goldsmiths, repaired the wall. Next to him, Hananiah son of the perfume maker repaired the wall. They made Jerusalem strong as far as the Broad Wall.
9 Next to them, Rephaiah son of Hur repaired the wall. He was the leader of half of the district of Jerusalem.
10 Next to him, Jedaiah son of Harumaph repaired the wall in front of his house. Next to him, Hattush son of Hashabneiah repaired the wall.
11 Malchijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-moab repaired another part of the wall. They also repaired the Tower of the Ovens.
12 Next to them, Shallum son of Hallohesh repaired the wall. He was the leader of the other half of the district of Jerusalem. His daughters helped him.
13 Hanun and the people of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate. They rebuilt it and put its doors, locks, and bars in place. They also repaired a thousand cubits of wall as far as the Dung Gate.
14 Malchijah son of Rechab repaired the Dung Gate. He was the leader of the district of Beth-haccherem. He rebuilt it and put its doors, locks, and bars in place.

15 Shallun son of Col-hozeh repaired the Fountain Gate. He was the leader of the district of Mizpah. He rebuilt it, put a roof on it, and put its doors, locks, and bars in place. He also repaired the wall of the Pool of Shelah near the king's garden. He worked as far as the stairs that go down from the City of David.

16 After him, Nehemiah son of Azbuk repaired the wall. He was the leader of half of the district of Beth-zur. He worked up to a point across from the tombs of David. He worked as far as the man-made pool and the House of the Mighty.
17 Next to him, the Levites made repairs under Rehum son of Bani. Next to him, Hashabiah repaired the wall for his district. He was the leader of half of the district of Keilah.
18 Next to him, their fellow Levites made repairs under Binnui son of Henadad. He was the leader of the other half of the district of Keilah.
19 Next to him, Ezer son of Jeshua repaired another part of the wall. He was the leader of Mizpah. He repaired the part across from the road up to the Armory, near the corner of the wall.
20 Next to him, Baruch son of Zabbai carefully repaired another part of the wall. He worked from the corner to the door of the house of Eliashib the high priest.
21 Next to him, Meremoth son of Uriah, the son of Hakkoz, repaired another part. He worked from the door of the house of Eliashib to the end of the house.
22 Next to him, the priests from the area around Jerusalem made repairs.
23 After them, Benjamin and Hasshub made repairs in front of their own house. Next to them, Azariah son of Maaseiah, the son of Ananiah, made repairs beside his house.
24 After him, Binnui son of Henadad repaired another part. He worked from the house of Azariah to the corner and the angle.

25 Palal son of Uzai made repairs across from the corner. He also worked at the tower that sticks out from the upper part of the king's palace, near the yard of the guard. Next to him, Pedaiah son of Parosh made repairs.
26 The temple servants who lived on the hill of Ophel made repairs across from the Water Gate toward the east. They also worked at the tower that sticks out.
27 Next to them, the Tekoites repaired another part. They worked from a point across from the great tower that sticks out to the wall of Ophel.
28 Above the Horse Gate, each priest made repairs in front of his own house.
29 Next to them, Zadok son of Immer made repairs across from his house. Next to him, Shemaiah son of Shecaniah, the guard of the East Gate, made repairs.
30 Next to him, Hananiah son of Shelemiah and Hanun the sixth son of Zalaph repaired another part. Next to them, Meshullam son of Berechiah made repairs across from his own rooms.

31 Next to him, Malchijah, one of the goldsmiths, made repairs. He worked as far as the house of the temple servants and the traders, across from the Inspection Gate. He worked as far as the upper room at the corner.
32 And between the upper room at the corner and the Sheep Gate, the goldsmiths and traders made repairs.

title: Nehemiah 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 When Sanballat heard that we were building the wall again, he became very angry. He made fun of the Jews.

2 In front of his friends and the army of Samaria, he said, "What are these weak Jews doing? Can they fix the wall alone? Will they make offerings to God? Will they finish it in one day? Can they bring these burned stones back from the piles of broken stones?"
3 Tobiah the Ammonite stood beside him and said, "Even if a fox climbed on their wall, the stones would fall down!"
4 Hear us, O God, for people look down on us. Turn their insults back on their own heads. Let them be taken away to a land of captivity.
5 Do not cover their guilt. Do not wipe their sin away from Your eyes. They have made the builders angry.
6 So we built the wall until it reached half its height. The people wanted to work.
7 Sanballat, Tobiah, the Arabs, the Ammonites, and the Ashdodites heard that the wall of Jerusalem was getting better. The gaps were closing. They became very angry.
8 They all made a plan together to come and fight Jerusalem and cause trouble.
9 So we prayed to our God. We placed a guard to watch day and night.
10 Then the people of Judah said, "The workers are losing their strength. There are too many broken stones. We cannot build the wall."
11 Our enemies said, "Before they see or know, we will come in among them. We will kill them and stop the work."
12 The Jews who lived near them came to us ten times. They said, "Wherever you turn, they will attack us."
13 So I placed men behind the lowest parts of the wall, at the weak places. I placed them by families with their swords, spears, and bows.

14 After I looked around, I stood up. I said to the leaders, the officers, and the rest of the people, "Do not be afraid of them. Remember the Lord, who is great and powerful. Fight for your brothers, your sons, your daughters, your wives, and your homes."
15 Our enemies heard that we knew their plan. They heard that God had stopped it. Each of us went back to our own work on the wall.
16 From that day, half of my servants did the work. The other half held spears, shields, bows, and armor. The officers stood behind all the people of Judah
17 who were building the wall. The workers who carried things worked with one hand. They held a weapon in the other hand.
18 Each builder worked with his sword on his side. The horn-blower stayed beside me.
19 Then I said to the leaders, the officers, and the rest of the people, "The work is big and wide. We are far from each other along the wall.
20 Wherever you hear the sound of the horn, come to us quickly there. Our God will fight for us!"
21 So we kept working. Half of the men held spears from the first light of morning until the stars came out.
22 At that time I also said to the people, "Let every man and his servant stay the night inside Jerusalem. Then they can guard at night and work in the day."
23 So my brothers, my servants, the guards with me, and I did not change our clothes. Each one kept his weapon, even when going for water.

title: Nehemiah 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 At that time the people and their wives made a loud complaint against their fellow Jews.
2 Some said, "We have many sons and daughters. We need grain so we can eat and live."
3 Others said, "We are giving our fields, vineyards, and homes as a pledge to get grain in this time of no food."
4 Still others said, "We have borrowed money to pay the king's tax on our fields and vineyards.

5 We are the same flesh as our countrymen, and our children are like their children. Yet we are making our sons and daughters slaves. Some of our daughters are already slaves. We have no power to buy them back, because our fields and vineyards belong to others."
6 When I heard their complaint and these words, I became very angry.
7 After I thought carefully, I spoke against the rich leaders and officers. I said, "You are charging high interest from your own brothers!" Then I called a large meeting against them.

8 I said, "We have done our best to buy back our Jewish brothers who were sold to foreign people. But now you are selling your own brothers, so we must buy them again!" They were silent, for they had nothing to say.
9 So I went on, "What you are doing is not right. Should you not walk in the fear of our God, so our foreign enemies cannot shame us?
10 My brothers, my servants, and I have also been lending money and grain to the people. Please, let us stop charging interest.
11 Please give back to them today their fields, vineyards, olive groves, and houses. Also give back the share of the money, grain, new wine, and oil that you have charged them."

12 They answered, "We will give it back, and we will ask nothing more from them. We will do as you say." So I called the priests, and I made the rich leaders and officers take a strong promise to do this.

13 I also shook out my robe and said, "May God shake out from His house every man who does not keep this promise. May he be shaken out and have nothing!" The whole meeting said, "Amen," and they praised the LORD. And the people did as they had promised.

14 Also, from the day King Artaxerxes made me governor in the land of Judah, from his twentieth year to his thirty-second year (twelve years in all), my brothers and I did not eat the food given to the governor.

15 The governors before me had put heavy loads on the people. They took bread and wine from them, plus forty shekels of silver. Their servants also pressed down on the people. But I did not do this, because of my fear of God.
16 Instead, I gave myself to the work of the wall. All my servants joined me there for the work, and we did not buy any land.
17 At my table there were 150 Jews and officers, besides the guests from the nations around us.

18 Each day one ox, six good sheep, and some birds were prepared for me. Every ten days a lot of wine of all kinds was brought in. Yet I did not ask for the food given to the governor, because the load on the people was so heavy.
19 Remember me with favor, O my God, for all I have done for this people.

title: Nehemiah 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 Sanballat, Tobiah, Geshem the Arab, and our other enemies heard the news. I had finished building the wall again. No broken place was left. But I had not yet put the doors in the gates.
2 Then Sanballat and Geshem sent me a message. They said, "Come, let us meet in one of the villages on the plain of Ono." But they wanted to hurt me.
3 So I sent messengers back to them. I said, "I am doing a great work. I cannot come down. Why should the work stop while I leave it to go to you?"
4 Four times they sent me the same message. Each time I gave them the same answer.
5 The fifth time, Sanballat sent his young servant with the same message. In his hand he held an open letter, not closed.

6 The letter said, "It is being said among the nations, and Geshem agrees, that you and the Jews are making secret plans to fight the king. That is why you are building the wall. People say you want to become their king.
7 They say you have even chosen prophets in Jerusalem to speak for you. They say, 'There is a king in Judah.' Soon these reports will reach the king. So come, let us talk together."
8 Then I sent him this reply: "None of what you say is true. You are making it up in your own head."
9 They were all trying to scare us. They said, "Their hands will grow weak, and the work will never be finished." But now, my God, make my hands strong.

10 Later I went to the house of Shemaiah son of Delaiah, the son of Mehetabel. He was shut up inside his house. He said, "Let us meet at the house of God, inside the temple. Let us shut the temple doors. They are coming to kill you. By night they are coming to kill you!"
11 But I answered, "Should a man like me run away? Should one like me go into the temple to save his own life? I will not go!"
12 Then I saw that God had not sent him. He had spoken this prophecy against me because Tobiah and Sanballat had paid him.
13 They paid him to scare me, so I would sin by doing what he said. Then they could give me a bad name and shame me.
14 O my God, remember Tobiah and Sanballat for what they have done. Remember also Noadiah the prophetess and the other prophets who tried to scare me.
15 So the wall was finished in fifty-two days, on the twenty-fifth day of Elul.
16 When all our enemies heard about this, all the nations around us were afraid. They lost their courage. They saw that this work had been done by our God.
17 Also in those days, the leading men of Judah sent many letters to Tobiah. And Tobiah's letters came back to them.
18 Many in Judah were tied to him by a promise. He was a son-in-law of Shecaniah son of Arah. His son Jehohanan had married the daughter of Meshullam son of Berechiah.
19 These leading men also kept telling me about Tobiah's good deeds. And they passed my words on to him. And Tobiah sent letters to scare me.

title: Nehemiah 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 The wall was finished. I set the doors in place. Then the gatekeepers, singers, and Levites were chosen.
2 I put my brother Hanani in charge of Jerusalem. I also put Hananiah, the leader of the strong tower, with him. He was a faithful man. He feared God more than most people.

3 I said to them, "Do not open the gates of Jerusalem until the sun is hot. While the guards are still working, keep the doors shut and locked tight. Choose people from Jerusalem as guards. Put some at their posts and some near their own homes."
4 The city was big and wide. But few people lived in it. And the houses had not yet been rebuilt.

5 Then my God put into my heart to gather the nobles, the leaders, and the people. I wanted to list them by family. I found the family record book of those who had first returned. This is what was written in it:
6 These are the people of the land. They came back from the group of captives. King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon had taken them away. They returned to Jerusalem and Judah, each to his own town.
7 They came with Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Azariah, Raamiah, Nahamani, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispereth, Bigvai, Nehum, and Baanah. This is the count of the men of Israel:
8 the descendants of Parosh, 2,172;
9 the descendants of Shephatiah, 372;
10 the descendants of Arah, 652;
11 the descendants of Pahath-moab (through the line of Jeshua and Joab), 2,818;
12 the descendants of Elam, 1,254;
13 the descendants of Zattu, 845;
14 the descendants of Zaccai, 760;
15 the descendants of Binnui, 648;
16 the descendants of Bebai, 628;
17 the descendants of Azgad, 2,322;
18 the descendants of Adonikam, 667;
19 the descendants of Bigvai, 2,067;
20 the descendants of Adin, 655;
21 the descendants of Ater (through Hezekiah), 98;
22 the descendants of Hashum, 328;
23 the descendants of Bezai, 324;
24 the descendants of Hariph, 112;
25 the descendants of Gibeon, 95;
26 the men of Bethlehem and Netophah, 188;
27 the men of Anathoth, 128;
28 the men of Beth-azmaveth, 42;
29 the men of Kiriath-jearim, Chephirah, and Beeroth, 743;
30 the men of Ramah and Geba, 621;
31 the men of Michmash, 122;
32 the men of Bethel and Ai, 123;
33 the men of the other Nebo, 52;
34 the descendants of the other Elam, 1,254;
35 the descendants of Harim, 320;
36 the men of Jericho, 345;
37 the men of Lod, Hadid, and Ono, 721;
38 and the descendants of Senaah, 3,930.
39 The priests: the descendants of Jedaiah (through the house of Jeshua), 973;
40 the descendants of Immer, 1,052;
41 the descendants of Pashhur, 1,247;
42 and the descendants of Harim, 1,017.
43 The Levites: the descendants of Jeshua (through Kadmiel, through the line of Hodevah), 74.
44 The singers: the descendants of Asaph, 148.
45 The gatekeepers: the descendants of Shallum, the descendants of Ater, the descendants of Talmon, the descendants of Akkub, the descendants of Hatita, and the descendants of Shobai, 138 in all.
46 The temple servants: the descendants of Ziha, the descendants of Hasupha, the descendants of Tabbaoth,
47 the descendants of Keros, the descendants of Sia, the descendants of Padon,
48 the descendants of Lebanah, the descendants of Hagabah, the descendants of Shalmai,
49 the descendants of Hanan, the descendants of Giddel, the descendants of Gahar,
50 the descendants of Reaiah, the descendants of Rezin, the descendants of Nekoda,
51 the descendants of Gazzam, the descendants of Uzza, the descendants of Paseah,
52 the descendants of Besai, the descendants of Meunim, the descendants of Nephushesim,
53 the descendants of Bakbuk, the descendants of Hakupha, the descendants of Harhur,
54 the descendants of Bazlith, the descendants of Mehida, the descendants of Harsha,
55 the descendants of Barkos, the descendants of Sisera, the descendants of Temah,
56 the descendants of Neziah, and the descendants of Hatipha.
57 The descendants of the servants of Solomon: the descendants of Sotai, the descendants of Sophereth, the descendants of Perida,
58 the descendants of Jaala, the descendants of Darkon, the descendants of Giddel,
59 the descendants of Shephatiah, the descendants of Hattil, the descendants of Pochereth-hazzebaim, and the descendants of Amon.
60 The temple servants and descendants of the servants of Solomon were 392 in all.
61 These men came up from Tel-melah, Tel-harsha, Cherub, Addon, and Immer. But they could not prove that their families came from Israel:
62 the descendants of Delaiah, the descendants of Tobiah, and the descendants of Nekoda, 642 in all.

63 And from among the priests: the descendants of Hobaiah, the descendants of Hakkoz, and the descendants of Barzillai. (Barzillai had married a daughter of Barzillai the Gileadite and was called by their name.)
64 These men looked for their family records. But they could not find them. So they were shut out from the priesthood as unclean.
65 The governor told them not to eat the most holy food. They had to wait until a priest could ask with the Urim and Thummim.
66 The whole group of people was 42,360.
67 Their menservants and maidservants were 7,337. They also had 245 male and female singers.
68 They had 736 horses and 245 mules,
69 435 camels, and 6,720 donkeys.
70 Some of the family leaders gave to help the work. The governor gave to the money store 1,000 darics of gold, 50 bowls, and 530 priestly robes.
71 Some of the family leaders gave to the money store for the work 20,000 darics of gold and 2,200 minas of silver.
72 The rest of the people gave 20,000 darics of gold, 2,000 minas of silver, and 67 priestly robes.

73 So the priests, Levites, gatekeepers, singers, and temple servants lived in their own towns. Some of the people and the rest of the Israelites lived there too. By the seventh month, the Israelites had settled in their towns.

title: Nehemiah 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 At that time all the people came together in the open place in front of the Water Gate. They asked Ezra the scribe to bring out the Book of the Law of Moses, which the LORD had given to Israel.
2 On the first day of the seventh month, Ezra the priest brought the Law before the group of men and women and all who could listen and understand.

3 Ezra read it out loud from early morning until noon. He faced the open place in front of the Water Gate. The men, women, and all who could understand listened. All the people listened with full attention to the Book of the Law.

4 Ezra the scribe stood on a high wooden stand made for this day. On his right side stood Mattithiah, Shema, Anaiah, Uriah, Hilkiah, and Maaseiah. On his left stood Pedaiah, Mishael, Malchijah, Hashum, Hash-baddanah, Zechariah, and Meshullam.
5 Ezra opened the book so everyone could see, because he stood above them. As he opened it, all the people stood up.
6 Then Ezra blessed the LORD, the great God. With their hands raised high, all the people said, "Amen, Amen!" Then they bowed low and worshiped the LORD with their faces to the ground.
7 The Levites—Jeshua, Bani, Sherebiah, Jamin, Akkub, Shabbethai, Hodiah, Maaseiah, Kelita, Azariah, Jozabad, Hanan, and Pelaiah—taught the people the Law as they stood in their places.
8 They read from the Book of the Law of God. They explained it and helped the people see the meaning, so the people could understand what was read.

9 Nehemiah the governor, Ezra the priest and scribe, and the Levites who were teaching the people spoke to them all. They said, "This day is holy to the LORD your God. Do not be sad or cry." For all the people were crying as they heard the words of the Law.

10 Then Nehemiah told them, "Go and eat rich food. Drink sweet drinks. Share food with those who have nothing ready. Today is holy to our Lord. Do not be sad, for the joy of the LORD is your strength."
11 And the Levites calmed all the people. They said, "Be quiet, for today is holy. Do not be sad."
12 Then all the people went to eat and drink. They shared food and were very happy, because they understood the words that had been taught to them.
13 On the second day of the month, the family leaders, with the priests and Levites, came to Ezra the scribe to study the words of the Law.
14 They found it written in the Law, which the LORD had given through Moses, that the Israelites were to live in booths during the feast of the seventh month.

15 So they announced this message in all their towns and in Jerusalem. They said, "Go out to the hills. Bring back branches of olive, wild olive, myrtle, palm, and other leafy trees. Make booths, as it is written."

16 So the people went out, brought back branches, and made booths. They built them on their own flat roofs, in their open yards, in the court of the house of God, in the open place by the Water Gate, and by the Gate of Ephraim.
17 All the group who had come back from exile made booths and lived in them. From the days of Joshua son of Nun until that day, the Israelites had not celebrated like this. And there was great joy.

18 Day after day, from the first day to the last, Ezra read from the Book of the Law of God. The Israelites kept the feast for seven days. On the eighth day they held a holy meeting, as the rule said.

title: Nehemiah 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 On day twenty-four of the same month, the Israelites came together. They went without food. They wore sackcloth. They put dust on their heads.
2 Those of Israelite family stood apart from all foreigners. They confessed their sins and the wrong acts of their fathers.
3 They stood in their places. For a quarter of the day they read from the Book of the Law of the LORD their God. For another quarter they confessed and worshiped the LORD their God.
4 The Levites—Jeshua, Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, and Chenani—stood on the raised platform. They cried out in a loud voice to the LORD their God.

5 Then the Levites—Jeshua, Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabneiah, Sherebiah, Hodiah, Shebaniah, and Pethahiah—said, "Stand up and bless the LORD your God from forever to forever. Blessed be Your glorious name. May it be lifted high above all blessing and praise.

6 You alone are the LORD. You made the heavens, the highest heavens with all their host, the earth and all on it, the seas and all in them. You give life to all things. The host of heaven worships You.
7 You are the LORD, the God who chose Abram. You brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans. You gave him the name Abraham.

8 You found his heart faithful before You. You made a covenant with him. You promised the land of the Canaanites and Hittites, of the Amorites and Perizzites, of the Jebusites and Girgashites—to give it to his children. You have kept Your promise, because You are just.
9 You saw the suffering of our fathers in Egypt. You heard their cry at the Red Sea.
10 You did signs and wonders against Pharaoh, all his officials, and all his people. For You knew they were proud against our fathers. You made a name for Yourself that lasts to this day.
11 You divided the sea before them. They crossed through it on dry ground. You threw their chasers into the deep, like a stone into wild waters.
12 You led them with a pillar of cloud by day. By night You led them with a pillar of fire, to light their way.
13 You came down on Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven. You gave them fair rules, true laws, and good commands.
14 You showed them Your holy Sabbath. You gave them commands and laws through Your servant Moses.
15 In their hunger You gave them bread from heaven. In their thirst You brought them water from the rock. You told them to go in and take the land You had sworn to give them.
16 But they and our fathers became proud and stubborn. They did not obey Your commands.

17 They refused to listen. They forgot the wonders You did among them. They became stubborn and chose a leader to send them back to slavery in Egypt. But You are a forgiving God, gracious and merciful, slow to anger and rich in steady love. You did not leave them.
18 They even made for themselves an image of a calf. They said, 'This is your god who brought you up out of Egypt.' They spoke terrible words against God.
19 Still, in Your great mercy, You did not leave them in the wilderness. By day the pillar of cloud never stopped guiding them. By night the pillar of fire lit the way they should go.
20 You gave Your good Spirit to teach them. You did not hold back Your manna from their mouths. You gave them water for their thirst.
21 For forty years You cared for them in the wilderness. They lacked nothing. Their clothes did not wear out. Their feet did not swell.
22 You gave them kingdoms and peoples. You gave them every corner of the land. So they took the land of Sihon king of Heshbon, and of Og king of Bashan.
23 You made their children many, like the stars of heaven. You brought them to the land You had told their fathers to enter and take.
24 So their children went in and took the land. You took control of the Canaanites in the land before them. You handed over to them the kings and peoples of the land, to do with them as they wished.

25 They captured strong cities and good land. They took houses full of every good thing, wells already dug, vineyards, olive trees, and fruit trees in plenty. So they ate and were full. They grew fat. They enjoyed Your great goodness.
26 But they were not obedient. They turned against You. They threw Your law behind their backs. They killed Your prophets, who had warned them to return to You. They spoke terrible words against God.

27 So You handed them over to enemies who hurt them. In their time of trouble they cried out to You. From heaven You heard them. In Your great mercy You gave them rescuers who saved them from their enemies.

28 But as soon as they had rest, they did evil again in Your sight. So You left them to their enemies, who ruled over them. When they cried out to You again, You heard from heaven. You rescued them many times in Your mercy.

29 You warned them to turn back to Your law. But they were proud and would not obey Your commands. They sinned against Your rules, by which a person will live if he keeps them. They stubbornly shrugged their shoulders. They became stubborn and would not obey.
30 You were patient with them for many years. Your Spirit warned them through Your prophets. Yet they would not listen. So You gave them into the hands of nearby peoples.
31 But in Your great mercy, You did not end them. You did not leave them. For You are a gracious and merciful God.

32 So now, our God, the great and mighty and awesome God who keeps His gracious covenant, do not see as small all the hard times that have come upon us. They have come upon our kings and leaders, our priests and prophets, our fathers and all Your people, from the days of the kings of Assyria until today.
33 You are just in all that has come on us. You have acted faithfully, while we have acted in evil ways.
34 Our kings and leaders and priests and fathers did not obey Your law. They did not listen to Your commands and warnings that You gave them.
35 Even while they were in their kingdom, with the great goodness You gave them, and in the wide and good land You set before them, they would not serve You. They would not turn from their evil ways.
36 So here we are today as slaves in the land You gave our fathers to enjoy. We are slaves in the land that should give us its fruit and goodness.
37 Its rich harvest goes to the kings You have set over us because of our sins. They rule over our bodies and our animals as they please. We are in great trouble.
38 Because of all this, we make a firm agreement. We put it in writing. We seal it with the names of our leaders, Levites, and priests."

title: Nehemiah 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 These men put their seal on the agreement: Nehemiah the governor, son of Hacaliah, and also Zedekiah,
2 Seraiah, Azariah, Jeremiah,
3 Pashhur, Amariah, Malchijah,
4 Hattush, Shebaniah, Malluch,
5 Harim, Meremoth, Obadiah,
6 Daniel, Ginnethon, Baruch,
7 Meshullam, Abijah, Mijamin,
8 Maaziah, Bilgai, and Shemaiah. These were the priests.
9 The Levites were: Jeshua son of Azaniah, Binnui of the sons of Henadad, and Kadmiel.
10 Their fellow Levites were: Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,
11 Mica, Rehob, Hashabiah,
12 Zaccur, Sherebiah, Shebaniah,
13 Hodiah, Bani, and Beninu.
14 The leaders of the people were: Parosh, Pahath-moab, Elam, Zattu, Bani,
15 Bunni, Azgad, Bebai,
16 Adonijah, Bigvai, Adin,
17 Ater, Hezekiah, Azzur,
18 Hodiah, Hashum, Bezai,
19 Hariph, Anathoth, Nebai,
20 Magpiash, Meshullam, Hezir,
21 Meshezabel, Zadok, Jaddua,
22 Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,
23 Hoshea, Hananiah, Hasshub,
24 Hallohesh, Pilha, Shobek,
25 Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,
26 Ahijah, Hanan, Anan,
27 Malluch, Harim, and Baanah.

28 The rest of the people joined them too. These were the priests, Levites, gatekeepers, singers, and temple servants. They had separated themselves from the other peoples of the land in order to obey the Law of God. Their wives and all their sons and daughters who could understand joined them as well.

29 They joined their noble brothers and made a solemn promise. They swore to follow the Law of God given through His servant Moses. They promised to obey all the commands, rules, and laws of the LORD our Lord.
30 "We will not give our daughters in marriage to the peoples of the land. We will not take their daughters as wives for our sons.

31 When the peoples of the land bring goods or grain to sell on the Sabbath, we will not buy from them on the Sabbath or on any holy day. Every seventh year we will not plant our fields, and we will forgive every debt.
32 We also accept this duty: each year we will give a third of a shekel for the service of the house of our God.

33 This will pay for the bread of the Presence (showbread), the regular grain offerings and burnt offerings, the Sabbath offerings, the New Moon and feast offerings, the holy offerings, the sin offerings to make atonement for Israel, and all the work of the house of our God.

34 We have drawn lots among the priests, Levites, and people. Each family must bring wood to the house of our God at set times each year. The wood will burn on the altar of the LORD our God, as the Law commands.
35 Each year we will bring the firstfruits of our land and of every fruit tree to the house of the LORD.
36 We will also bring the firstborn of our sons and our livestock, as the Law commands. We will bring the firstborn of our herds and flocks to the house of our God, to the priests who serve there.

37 We will bring to the priests, at the storerooms of the house of our God, the firstfruits of our dough, of our grain offerings, of every kind of fruit, and of our new wine and oil. We will give a tenth (tithe) of our crops to the Levites, since the Levites collect the tithe in all the towns where we work.
38 A priest from Aaron's family must go with the Levites when they collect the tithe. The Levites must then bring a tenth of these tithes to the treasury storerooms in the house of our God.

39 The Israelites and the Levites must bring the gifts of grain, new wine, and oil to the storerooms. The sacred vessels are kept there. The serving priests, the gatekeepers, and the singers also stay there. So we will not forget the house of our God."

title: Nehemiah 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 Now the leaders of the people made their home in Jerusalem. The rest of the people drew lots. One out of every ten would live in the holy city of Jerusalem. The other nine could live in their own towns.
2 And the people blessed all the men who chose to live in Jerusalem.

3 These are the leaders of the regions who made their home in Jerusalem. But in the villages of Judah, each person lived on his own land. This included the Israelites, priests, Levites, temple workers, and the family line of Solomon's servants.

4 Some from the family line of Judah and Benjamin also made their home in Jerusalem. From the family line of Judah: Athaiah son of Uzziah. Uzziah was the son of Zechariah, the son of Amariah, the son of Shephatiah, the son of Mahalalel. He came from the family line of Perez.
5 And Maaseiah son of Baruch. Baruch was the son of Col-hozeh, the son of Hazaiah, the son of Adaiah, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zechariah. He came from the family line of Shelah.
6 The family line of Perez who lived in Jerusalem was 468 brave men in total.
7 From the family line of Benjamin: Sallu son of Meshullam. Meshullam was the son of Joed, the son of Pedaiah, the son of Kolaiah, the son of Maaseiah, the son of Ithiel, the son of Jeshaiah.
8 And his followers Gabbai and Sallai—928 men in total.
9 Joel son of Zichri was the leader over them. Judah son of Hassenuah was in charge of the Second District of the city.
10 From the priests:

11 Jedaiah son of Joiarib; Jachin; and Seraiah son of Hilkiah. Hilkiah was the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub. Seraiah was the chief leader of the house of God.
12 Their fellow workers did the work at the temple—822 men.
13 Adaiah son of Jeroham. Jeroham was the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashhur, the son of Malchijah. His fellow workers were the leaders of families—242 men.
14 Amashai son of Azarel. Azarel was the son of Ahzai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer. His fellow workers were 128 brave men. Zabdiel son of Haggedolim was their leader in charge.
15 From the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub. Hasshub was the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Bunni.
16 Shabbethai and Jozabad were two leaders of the Levites. They watched over the work outside the house of God.

17 Mattaniah son of Mica. Mica was the son of Zabdi, the son of Asaph. Mattaniah led the people in thanks and prayer. Bakbukiah was second among his fellow workers. Abda son of Shammua. Shammua was the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun.
18 The Levites in the holy city were 284 in total.
19 And the gate guards: Akkub, Talmon, and their fellow workers. They kept watch at the gates—172 men.
20 The rest of the Israelites, with the priests and Levites, lived in all the villages of Judah. Each person lived on his own family land.
21 The temple workers lived on the hill of Ophel. Ziha and Gishpa were over them.

22 The leader in charge of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani. Bani was the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Mica. He came from the family line of Asaph. They were the singers who led the worship at the house of God.
23 The king gave a command about the singers. It was a rule about their daily work.
24 Pethahiah son of Meshezabel was the king's helper in every matter about the people. He came from the family line of Zerah son of Judah.
25 Some of the people of Judah lived in the villages with their fields. They lived in Kiriath-arba, Dibon, Jekabzeel, and the villages near them.
26 They lived in Jeshua, Moladah, and Beth-pelet.
27 They lived in Hazar-shual. They lived in Beersheba and its villages.
28 They lived in Ziklag. They lived in Meconah and its villages.
29 They lived in En-rimmon, Zorah, and Jarmuth.

30 They lived in Zanoah, Adullam, and the villages near them. They lived in Lachish and its fields. They lived in Azekah and its villages. So they made their home from Beersheba all the way to the Valley of Hinnom.
31 The family line of Benjamin from Geba lived in Michmash, Aija, and Bethel with its villages.
32 They lived in Anathoth, Nob, and Ananiah.
33 They lived in Hazor, Ramah, and Gittaim.
34 They lived in Hadid, Zeboim, and Neballat.
35 They lived in Lod and Ono. They lived in the Valley of the Craftsmen.
36 And some groups of the Levites from Judah moved to live in Benjamin.

title: Nehemiah 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 These are the priests and Levites who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and with Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
2 Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
3 Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
4 Iddo, Ginnethon, Abijah,
5 Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
6 Shemaiah, Joiarib, Jedaiah,
7 Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, and Jedaiah. These were the leaders of the priests in the days of Jeshua.
8 The Levites were Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, who led the thank-you songs with his group.
9 Bakbukiah and Unni, their helpers, stood across from them in the services.
10 Jeshua was the father of Joiakim. Joiakim was the father of Eliashib. Eliashib was the father of Joiada.
11 Joiada was the father of Jonathan. And Jonathan was the father of Jaddua.
12 In the days of Joiakim, these were the leaders of the priestly families: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
13 of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
14 of Malluchi, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
15 of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
16 of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
17 of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin and of Moadiah, Piltai;
18 of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jonathan;
19 of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
20 of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
21 of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; and of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
22 In the days of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua, the heads of the Levite and priestly families were written down. This was during the reign of Darius the Persian.
23 The Levite family heads up to the days of Johanan son of Eliashib were written in the Book of the Records.

24 The leaders of the Levites were Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their helpers. They stood across from each other to give praise and thanks, as David the man of God had commanded.
25 Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers. They guarded the store rooms at the gates.
26 They served in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua, son of Jozadak. They also served in the days of Nehemiah the governor and Ezra the priest and scribe.
27 At the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, the Levites were brought from all their homes. They came to Jerusalem to celebrate the dedication with joy, songs, cymbals, harps, and lyres.
28 The singers were also gathered from the region around Jerusalem, from the villages of the Netophathites,
29 from Beth-gilgal, and from the fields of Geba and Azmaveth. They had built villages for themselves around Jerusalem.
30 The priests and Levites made themselves clean. Then they made the people, the gates, and the wall clean.
31 Then I brought the leaders of Judah up on the wall. I appointed two large thank-you choirs. One was to walk along the top of the wall to the right, toward the Dung Gate.
32 Hoshaiah and half the leaders of Judah followed,
33 along with Azariah, Ezra, Meshullam,
34 Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, Jeremiah,
35 and some of the priests with trumpets. Zechariah son of Jonathan was also there, son of Shemaiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Micaiah, son of Zaccur, son of Asaph,
36 and his helpers — Shemaiah, Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, Judah, and Hanani — with the music instruments commanded by David the man of God. Ezra the scribe led the procession.
37 At the Fountain Gate they climbed the steps of the City of David, up to the wall. They passed above the house of David to the Water Gate on the east.
38 The second thank-you choir walked to the left. I followed it with half the people along the top of the wall. We passed the Tower of the Ovens to the Broad Wall,
39 over the Gate of Ephraim, the Jeshanah Gate, the Fish Gate, the Tower of Hananel, and the Tower of the Hundred, as far as the Sheep Gate. They stopped at the Gate of the Guard.
40 The two thank-you choirs then stood in the house of God. I stood there too, with half of the officials with me,
41 as well as the priests with their trumpets — Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah —
42 and also Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. Then the choirs sang out under the direction of Jezrahiah.
43 On that day they offered great sacrifices. They rejoiced because God had given them great joy. The women and children also rejoiced. The joy of Jerusalem was heard from far away.

44 On that same day men were appointed over the rooms that held the supplies, gifts, first fruits, and tithes. The portions set by the Law for the priests and Levites were brought there from the fields. Judah rejoiced over the priests and Levites who served.
45 They did the service of their God and the service of cleansing. The singers and gatekeepers did the same, as David and his son Solomon had commanded.
46 Long ago, in the days of David and Asaph, there were leaders for the singers. They led the songs of praise and thanks to God.

47 So in the days of Zerubbabel and Nehemiah, all Israel gave daily portions for the singers and gatekeepers. They also set aside daily portions for the Levites. And the Levites set aside daily portions for Aaron's family.

title: Nehemiah 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nehemiah
paragraphs: 1 At that time the Book of Moses was read aloud to the people. They found a passage saying no Ammonite or Moabite could ever enter the assembly of God,
2 because they had not met the Israelites with food and water. They had hired Balaam to call down a curse on them. But our God turned the curse into a blessing.
3 When the people heard this law, they removed from Israel all of foreign descent.
4 Before this, Eliashib the priest, a relative of Tobiah, was in charge of the store rooms of the house of our God.

5 He had prepared for Tobiah a large room. There they used to store the grain offerings, the frankincense, the temple articles, and the tithes of grain, new wine, and oil for the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers.
6 While all this was happening, I was not in Jerusalem. I had returned to Artaxerxes king of Babylon in the thirty-second year of his reign. Later I got permission from the king
7 to return to Jerusalem. Then I learned of the evil Eliashib had done for Tobiah by giving him a room in the courts of the house of God.
8 I was very upset and threw all of Tobiah's household goods out of the room.
9 Then I ordered that the rooms be made clean. I had the articles of the house of God brought back, along with the grain offerings and frankincense.
10 I also learned that the portions for the Levites had not been given. So all the Levites and singers had gone back to their own fields.
11 I rebuked the leaders and asked, "Why has the house of God been neglected?" Then I gathered the Levites and singers and put them back at their posts,
12 and all Judah brought a tenth of the grain, new wine, and oil into the store rooms.

13 I appointed as treasurers over the store rooms Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah the Levite. Hanan son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah, helped them, because they were trusted. They gave out the supplies to their fellow Levites.
14 Remember me for this, O my God, and do not wipe out my loving deeds for the house of my God and its services.

15 In those days I saw people in Judah treading winepresses on the Sabbath. They were bringing in grain and loading it on donkeys, along with wine, grapes, and figs. All kinds of goods were brought into Jerusalem on the Sabbath. So I warned them not to sell food on that day.
16 Men of Tyre who lived there were also importing fish and all kinds of goods. They were selling them on the Sabbath to the people of Judah in Jerusalem.
17 Then I rebuked the leaders of Judah. I asked, "What is this evil you are doing — making the Sabbath day unholy?
18 Did not your ancestors do the same things? Our God brought all this trouble on us and on this city. Now you are bringing his anger on Israel again by making the Sabbath unholy!"

19 When evening shadows began to fall on the gates of Jerusalem just before the Sabbath, I ordered the gates shut. They were not to open until after the Sabbath. I posted some of my servants at the gates so no load could enter on the Sabbath.
20 Once or twice the merchants and sellers of all kinds of goods camped outside Jerusalem,
21 but I warned them, "Why are you camping in front of the wall? If you do it again, I will lay hands on you." From that time on, they did not return on the Sabbath.
22 Then I told the Levites to make themselves clean and guard the gates to keep the Sabbath holy. Remember me for this also, O my God, and show me mercy by your great loving devotion.
23 In those days I also saw Jews who had married women from Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab.
24 Half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod or other peoples. They could not speak the language of Judah.

25 I rebuked them and called down curses on them. I beat some of these men and pulled out their hair. Then I made them swear before God, "You must not give your daughters to their sons or take their daughters as wives for your sons or for yourselves!

26 Did not King Solomon of Israel sin in things like this? There was no king like him among many nations. He was loved by his God, who made him king over all Israel. Yet foreign women led him into sin.
27 Must we now hear that you also are doing all this great evil? You are unfaithful to our God by marrying foreign women!"
28 Even one of the sons of Jehoiada son of Eliashib the high priest had become a son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite. So I drove him away from me.
29 Remember them, O my God, because they have made the priesthood unclean and broken the covenant of the priesthood and of the Levites.
30 So I made the priests and Levites clean from everything foreign. I assigned each of them his own duty.
31 I also arranged for wood gifts at the right times and for the first fruits. Remember me, O my God, with favor.
